origin.name: Belgium
origin.death_registration: Service état civil / Dienst Burgerlijke Stand (Civil Registry)
origin.investigating_authority: Procureur du Roi / Procureur des Konings (Prosecutor)
origin.key_document: Acte de décès / Overlijdensakte (death certificate from Civil Registry)
origin.source: FCDO Belgium travel advice, updated 2025; British Embassy Brussels guidance
origin.cremation_note: Cremation in Belgium is available. Bringing ashes home to the UK is often simpler than full body repatriation. You will need the death certificate, cremation certificate, and urn transport documentation.

destinations.name: United Kingdom
key_documents: Acte de décès or Overlijdensakte (death certificate)
Certified English translation of death certificate
special_rules: Belgium is an EU member and Hague Apostille Convention member, which simplifies document legalisation.
Death certificates may be issued in French or Dutch depending on the region of Belgium.
All French or Dutch-language documentation requires certified English translation for UK acceptance.
The Procureur orders post-mortem for suspicious or violent deaths.
destinations.dest_reception: The UK funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. All Belgian documentation must be in certified English translation. The coroner for the district is notified. Straightforward cases proceed directly to funeral arrangements.
destinations.dest_consular_note: FCDO 24-hour emergency line: +44 (0)20 7008 5000. The British Embassy in Brussels can register the death and advise on local funeral directors. They cannot pay for or arrange repatriation.
destinations.seo_title: Belgium to UK Repatriation: Guidance for Families
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Belgium. Repatriation to the UK takes 1-2 weeks. Civil registry, apostille, and air cargo all handled. Contact us 24/7.
